results.question: Watson describes Holmes between cases as alternating between two contrasting states. When Jabez Wilson brings the Red-Headed League problem, Holmes reacts very differently to having a puzzle in front of him. What are the two states Watson describes Holmes cycling through in the Scandal in Bohemia opening, and what specific phrase does Holmes use in the Red-Headed League case to signal he is now fully engaged?
